1→//! Shared helpers for OpenAI-compatible streaming with tools.
     2→//!
     3→//! This module provides reusable functions for parsing OpenAI-style SSE streams
     4→//! that include tool calls. Used by openai.rs, github_copilot.rs, and kimi_code.rs.
     5→
     6→use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
     7→
     8→use {
     9→    moltis_agents::model::{
    10→        ChatMessage, CompletionResponse, StreamEvent, ToolCall, Usage, UserContent,
    11→        decode_tool_call_arguments,
    12→    },
    13→    serde::Serialize,
    14→    tracing::trace,
    15→};
    16→
    17→use super::{
    18→    schema_normalization::sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat,
    19→    strict_mode::patch_schema_for_strict_mode,
    20→};
    21→
    22→// ============================================================================
    23→// OpenAI Tool Schema Types
    24→// ============================================================================
    25→// These types enforce the correct structure for OpenAI-compatible APIs.
    26→// Using typed structs instead of manual JSON prevents missing fields at compile time.
    27→//
    28→// References:
    29→// - Chat Completions: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
    30→// - Responses API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/openai/how-to/responses
    31→// ============================================================================
    32→
    33→/// Chat Completions API tool format (nested under "function").
    34→///
    35→/// ```json
    36→/// { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "...", ... } }
    37→/// ```
    38→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    39→pub struct ChatCompletionsTool {
    40→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    41→    pub tool_type: &'static str,
    42→    pub function: ChatCompletionsFunction,
    43→}
    44→
    45→/// The function definition nested inside ChatCompletionsTool.
    46→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    47→pub struct ChatCompletionsFunction {
    48→    pub name: String,
    49→    pub description: String,
    50→    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    51→    pub strict: bool,
    52→}
    53→
    54→/// Responses API tool format (flat, name at top level).
    55→///
    56→/// ```json
    57→/// { "type": "function", "name": "...", "parameters": {...}, "strict": true }
    58→/// ```
    59→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    60→pub struct ResponsesApiTool {
    61→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    62→    pub tool_type: &'static str,
    63→    pub name: String,
    64→    pub description: String,
    65→    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    66→    pub strict: bool,
    67→}
    68→
    69→/// Convert tool schemas to OpenAI Chat Completions function-calling format.
    70→///
    71→/// Uses the nested `function` object format required by Chat Completions API:
    72→/// ```json
    73→/// { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "...", ... } }
    74→/// ```
    75→///
    76→/// When `strict` is `true`, patches schemas for strict mode compliance:
    77→/// - `additionalProperties: false` on all object schemas
    78→/// - All properties included in `required` array
    79→/// - Originally-optional properties made nullable via array-form types
    80→///
    81→/// When `strict` is `false`, schemas are sanitized but not patched for strict
    82→/// mode. This avoids array-form types like `["boolean", "null"]` that
    83→/// non-OpenAI backends (e.g. Google via OpenRouter) reject.
    84→///
    85→/// See: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling>
    86→pub fn to_openai_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value], strict: bool) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
    87→    let result: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tools
    88→        .iter()
    89→        .filter_map(|t| {
    90→            let mut params = t["parameters"].clone();
    91→            sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params);
    92→            if strict {
    93→                patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params);
    94→            }
    95→
    96→            let name = t["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
    97→            let description = t["description"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
    98→
    99→            // Use typed struct to ensure all required fields are present
   100→            let tool = ChatCompletionsTool {
   101→                tool_type: "function",
   102→                function: ChatCompletionsFunction {
   103→                    name: name.clone(),
   104→                    description,
   105→                    parameters: params,
   106→                    strict,
   107→                },
   108→            };
   109→
   110→            trace!(tool_name = %name, strict, "converted tool to Chat Completions format");
   111→
   112→            // Serialize to Value for compatibility with existing API
   113→            serde_json::to_value(tool).ok()
   114→        })
   115→        .collect();
   116→
   117→    trace!(tools_count = result.len(), "to_openai_tools complete");
   118→    result
   119→}
   120→
   121→/// Convert tool schemas to OpenAI Responses API function-calling format.
   122→///
   123→/// Uses the flat format required by the Responses API where `name` is at top level:
   124→/// ```json
   125→/// { "type": "function", "name": "...", "parameters": {...}, "strict": true }
   126→/// ```
   127→///
   128→/// This is the format used by OpenAI Codex and the Responses API.
   129→///
   130→/// Patches schemas for strict mode compliance:
   131→/// - `additionalProperties: false` on all object schemas
   132→/// - All properties included in `required` array
   133→///
   134→/// See: <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/openai/how-to/responses>
   135→pub fn to_responses_api_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value]) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
   136→    let result: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tools
   137→        .iter()
   138→        .filter_map(|t| {
   139→            // Clone parameters and patch for strict mode
   140→            let mut params = t["parameters"].clone();
   141→            sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params);
   142→            patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params);
   143→
   144→            let name = t["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
   145→            let description = t["description"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
   146→
   147→            // Use typed struct to ensure all required fields are present
   148→            let tool = ResponsesApiTool {
   149→                tool_type: "function",
   150→                name: name.clone(),
   151→                description,
   152→                parameters: params,
   153→                strict: true,
   154→            };
   155→
   156→            trace!(tool_name = %name, "converted tool to Responses API format");
   157→
   158→            // Serialize to Value for compatibility with existing API
   159→            serde_json::to_value(tool).ok()
   160→        })
   161→        .collect();
   162→
   163→    trace!(
   164→        tools_count = result.len(),
   165→        "to_responses_api_tools complete"
   166→    );
   167→    result
   168→}
   169→
   170→/// Convert typed chat messages to Responses API input items.
   171→///
   172→/// Responses API accepts a heterogeneous input array (messages, tool calls, and
   173→/// tool outputs). This keeps one canonical conversion for providers that use
   174→/// Responses transport (SSE or WebSocket).
   175→#[must_use]
   176→pub fn to_responses_input(messages: &[ChatMessage]) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
   177→    messages
   178→        .iter()
   179→        .flat_map(|msg| match msg {
   180→            ChatMessage::System { .. } => {
   181→                // System messages are extracted into `instructions`.
   182→                vec![]
   183→            },
   184→            ChatMessage::User { content, .. } => {
   185→                let content_blocks = match content {
   186→                    UserContent::Text(t) => {
   187→                        vec![serde_json::json!({"type": "input_text", "text": t})]
   188→                    },
   189→                    UserContent::Multimodal(parts) => parts
   190→                        .iter()
   191→                        .map(|p| match p {
   192→                            moltis_agents::model::ContentPart::Text(t) => {
   193→                                serde_json::json!({"type": "input_text", "text": t})
   194→                            },
   195→                            moltis_agents::model::ContentPart::Image { media_type, data } => {
   196→                                let data_uri = format!("data:{media_type};base64,{data}");
   197→                                serde_json::json!({
   198→                                    "type": "input_image",
   199→                                    "image_url": data_uri,
   200→                                })
   201→                            },
   202→                        })
   203→                        .collect(),
   204→                };
   205→                vec![serde_json::json!({
   206→                    "role": "user",
   207→                    "content": content_blocks,
   208→                })]
   209→            },
   210→            ChatMessage::Assistant {
   211→                content,
   212→                tool_calls,
   213→            } => {
   214→                if !tool_calls.is_empty() {
   215→                    let mut items: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tool_calls
   216→                        .iter()
   217→                        .map(|tc| {
   218→                            serde_json::json!({
   219→                                "type": "function_call",
   220→                                "call_id": tc.id,
   221→                                "name": tc.name,
   222→                                "arguments": tc.arguments.to_string(),
   223→                            })
   224→                        })
   225→                        .collect();
   226→                    if let Some(text) = content
   227→                        && !text.is_empty()
   228→                    {
   229→                        items.insert(
   230→                            0,
   231→                            serde_json::json!({
   232→                                "type": "message",
   233→                                "role": "assistant",
   234→                                "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": text}]
   235→                            }),
   236→                        );
   237→                    }
   238→                    items
   239→                } else {
   240→                    let text = content.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
   241→                    vec![serde_json::json!({
   242→                        "type": "message",
   243→                        "role": "assistant",
   244→                        "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": text}]
   245→                    })]
   246→                }
   247→            },
   248→            ChatMessage::Tool {
   249→                tool_call_id,
   250→                content,
   251→            } => {
   252→                vec![serde_json::json!({
   253→                    "type": "function_call_output",
   254→                    "call_id": tool_call_id,
   255→                    "output": content,
   256→                })]
   257→            },
   258→        })
   259→        .collect()
   260→}
   261→
   262→/// Parse tool_calls from an OpenAI response message (non-streaming).
   263→pub fn parse_tool_calls(message: &serde_json::Value) -> Vec<ToolCall> {
   264→    message["tool_calls"]
   265→        .as_array()
   266→        .map(|tcs| {
   267→            tcs.iter()
   268→                .filter_map(|tc| {
   269→                    let id = tc["id"].as_str()?.to_string();
   270→                    let name = tc["function"]["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
   271→                    let arguments = decode_tool_call_arguments(tc["function"].get("arguments"));
   272→                    Some(ToolCall {
   273→                        id,
   274→                        name,
   275→                        arguments,
   276→                    })
   277→                })
   278→                .collect()
   279→        })
   280→        .unwrap_or_default()
   281→}
   282→
   283→fn usage_value_at_path(usage: &serde_json::Value, path: &[&str]) -> Option<u64> {
   284→    let mut cursor = usage;
   285→    for key in path {
   286→        cursor = cursor.get(*key)?;
   287→    }
   288→    cursor
   289→        .as_u64()
   290→        .or_else(|| cursor.as_str().and_then(|raw| raw.parse::<u64>().ok()))
   291→}
   292→
   293→fn usage_field_u32(usage: &serde_json::Value, paths: &[&[&str]]) -> u32 {
   294→    paths
   295→        .iter()
   296→        .find_map(|path| usage_value_at_path(usage, path))
   297→        .unwrap_or(0) as u32
   298→}

   300→fn usage_object_from_payload(payload: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<&serde_json::Value> {
   301→    if let Some(usage) = payload.get("usage").filter(|usage| usage.is_object()) {
   302→        return Some(usage);
   303→    }
   304→
   305→    if let Some(usage) = payload
   306→        .get("choices")
   307→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_array)
   308→        .and_then(|choices| choices.first())
   309→        .and_then(|choice| choice.get("usage"))
   310→        .filter(|usage| usage.is_object())
   311→    {
   312→        return Some(usage);
   313→    }
   314→
   315→    if let Some(usage) = payload
   316→        .get("choices")
   317→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_array)
   318→        .and_then(|choices| choices.first())
   319→        .and_then(|choice| choice.get("delta"))
   320→        .and_then(|delta| delta.get("usage"))
   321→        .filter(|usage| usage.is_object())
   322→    {
   323→        return Some(usage);
   324→    }
   325→
   326→    if let Some(usage) = payload
   327→        .get("choices")
   328→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_array)
   329→        .and_then(|choices| choices.first())
   330→        .and_then(|choice| choice.get("message"))
   331→        .and_then(|message| message.get("usage"))
   332→        .filter(|usage| usage.is_object())
   333→    {
   334→        return Some(usage);
   335→    }
   336→
   337→    payload
   338→        .get("x_groq")
   339→        .and_then(|x_groq| x_groq.get("usage"))
   340→        .filter(|usage| usage.is_object())
   341→}
   342→
   343→/// Parse usage payloads from OpenAI-compatible backends.
   344→///
   345→/// Different providers use different field names:
   346→/// - OpenAI-style: `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`
   347→/// - Anthropic/MiniMax-style: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`
   348→/// - Cache fields may be top-level or nested in `*_tokens_details`.
   349→#[must_use]
   350→pub fn parse_openai_compat_usage(usage: &serde_json::Value) -> Usage {
   351→    Usage {
   352→        input_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   353→            &["prompt_tokens"],
   354→            &["promptTokens"],
   355→            &["input_tokens"],
   356→            &["inputTokens"],
   357→        ]),
   358→        output_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   359→            &["completion_tokens"],
   360→            &["completionTokens"],
   361→            &["output_tokens"],
   362→            &["outputTokens"],
   363→        ]),
   364→        cache_read_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   365→            &["prompt_tokens_details", "cached_tokens"],
   366→            &["promptTokensDetails", "cachedTokens"],
   367→            &["input_tokens_details", "cached_tokens"],
   368→            &["inputTokensDetails", "cachedTokens"],
   369→            &["cache_read_input_tokens"],
   370→            &["cacheReadInputTokens"],
   371→            &["input_tokens_details", "cache_read_input_tokens"],
   372→            &["inputTokensDetails", "cacheReadInputTokens"],
   373→        ]),
   374→        cache_write_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   375→            &["cache_creation_input_tokens"],
   376→            &["cacheCreationInputTokens"],
   377→            &["input_tokens_details", "cache_creation_input_tokens"],
   378→            &["inputTokensDetails", "cacheCreationInputTokens"],
   379→        ]),
   380→    }
   381→}
   382→
   383→/// Parse usage from an OpenAI-compatible payload, checking common nesting variants.
   384→///
   385→/// Providers differ on where they place usage metadata:
   386→/// - top-level `usage`
   387→/// - `choices[0].usage`
   388→/// - `choices[0].delta.usage`
   389→/// - `choices[0].message.usage`
   390→/// - provider extension blocks (for example `x_groq.usage`)
   391→#[must_use]
   392→pub fn parse_openai_compat_usage_from_payload(payload: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<Usage> {
   393→    usage_object_from_payload(payload).map(parse_openai_compat_usage)
   394→}
   395→
   396→/// Strip `<think>...</think>` tags from content, returning `(visible, thinking)`.
   397→///
   398→/// Models like DeepSeek R1, QwQ, and MiniMax embed chain-of-thought reasoning
   399→/// inside `<think>` tags in the `content` field rather than using a separate
   400→/// `reasoning_content` field.  This helper splits content into the visible
   401→/// answer text and the thinking text so callers can handle them appropriately.
   402→///
   403→/// Edge cases handled:
   404→/// - Multiple `<think>` blocks interspersed with answer text
   405→/// - Unclosed `<think>` tag (remainder treated as reasoning)
   406→/// - Empty `<think></think>` blocks
   407→/// - Nested angle brackets inside thinking text
   408→pub fn strip_think_tags(content: &str) -> (String, String) {
   409→    let mut visible = String::new();
   410→    let mut thinking = String::new();
   411→    let mut remaining = content;
   412→
   413→    loop {
   414→        match remaining.find("<think>") {
   415→            Some(start) => {
   416→                // Text before <think> is visible
   417→                visible.push_str(&remaining[..start]);
   418→                let after_open = &remaining[start + "<think>".len()..];
   419→                match after_open.find("</think>") {
   420→                    Some(end) => {
   421→                        thinking.push_str(&after_open[..end]);
   422→                        remaining = &after_open[end + "</think>".len()..];
   423→                    },
   424→                    None => {
   425→                        // Unclosed <think> — treat rest as reasoning
   426→                        thinking.push_str(after_open);
   427→                        break;
   428→                    },
   429→                }
   430→            },
   431→            None => {
   432→                visible.push_str(remaining);
   433→                break;
   434→            },
   435→        }
   436→    }
   437→
   438→    (
   439→        visible.trim_start().to_string(),
   440→        thinking.trim_start().to_string(),
   441→    )
   442→}
   443→
   444→/// State for tracking streaming tool calls.
   445→#[derive(Default)]
   446→pub struct StreamingToolState {
   447→    /// Map from index -> (id, name, arguments_buffer)
   448→    pub tool_calls: HashMap<usize, (String, String, String)>,
   449→    pub input_tokens: u32,
   450→    pub output_tokens: u32,
   451→    pub cache_read_tokens: u32,
   452→    pub cache_write_tokens: u32,
   453→    /// Whether we are currently inside a `<think>` block in streamed content.
   454→    in_think_block: bool,
   455→    /// Whether we are still stripping leading whitespace at the start of a
   456→    /// think block. Set to `true` when entering `<think>`, cleared once
   457→    /// non-whitespace reasoning content is emitted.
   458→    think_strip_leading_ws: bool,
   459→    /// Whether we are still stripping leading whitespace from visible content
   460→    /// after exiting a `</think>` block. Models often emit `\n\n` between
   461→    /// `</think>` and the actual answer.
   462→    visible_strip_leading_ws: bool,
   463→    /// Buffer for detecting `<think>` / `</think>` tags that may be split
   464→    /// across SSE chunk boundaries.
   465→    tag_buffer: String,
   466→}
   467→
   468→/// Result of processing a single SSE line.
   469→#[derive(Debug)]
   470→pub enum SseLineResult {
   471→    /// No actionable event (empty line, non-data prefix)
   472→    Skip,
   473→    /// Stream is done
   474→    Done,
   475→    /// Events to yield
   476→    Events(Vec<StreamEvent>),
   477→}
   478→
   479→/// Emit a `ReasoningDelta`, stripping leading whitespace at the start of a
   480→/// think block so the UI doesn't show a blank prefix.
   481→fn emit_reasoning(text: String, strip_leading_ws: &mut bool, events: &mut Vec<StreamEvent>) {
   482→    if text.is_empty() {
   483→        return;
   484→    }
   485→    let emitted = if *strip_leading_ws {
   486→        let trimmed = text.trim_start();
   487→        if trimmed.is_empty() {
   488→            // Entire chunk was whitespace — keep stripping
   489→            return;
   490→        }
   491→        *strip_leading_ws = false;
   492→        trimmed.to_string()
   493→    } else {
   494→        text
   495→    };
   496→    events.push(StreamEvent::ReasoningDelta(emitted));
   497→}
   498→
   499→/// Emit a visible `Delta`, stripping leading whitespace after a `</think>`
   500→/// block so the UI doesn't show blank lines before the answer.
   501→fn emit_visible(text: String, strip_leading_ws: &mut bool, events: &mut Vec<StreamEvent>) {
   502→    if text.is_empty() {
   503→        return;
   504→    }
   505→    let emitted = if *strip_leading_ws {
   506→        let trimmed = text.trim_start();
   507→        if trimmed.is_empty() {
   508→            // Entire chunk was whitespace — keep stripping
   509→            return;
   510→        }
   511→        *strip_leading_ws = false;
   512→        trimmed.to_string()
   513→    } else {
   514→        text
   515→    };
   516→    events.push(StreamEvent::Delta(emitted));
   517→}
   518→
   519→/// Process streamed content through the `<think>` tag state machine.
   520→///
   521→/// Content arriving inside `<think>...</think>` is emitted as
   522→/// `ReasoningDelta`; content outside is emitted as `Delta`.
   523→/// Tags may be split across SSE chunks — `tag_buffer` accumulates
   524→/// partial tag fragments until they can be resolved.
   525→/// Leading whitespace at the start of each think block is stripped.
   526→fn process_content_think_tags(
   527→    content: &str,
   528→    state: &mut StreamingToolState,
   529→    events: &mut Vec<StreamEvent>,
   530→) {
   531→    state.tag_buffer.push_str(content);
   532→
   533→    loop {
   534→        if state.in_think_block {
   535→            // Look for </think> to exit think mode
   536→            match state.tag_buffer.find("</think>") {
   537→                Some(pos) => {
   538→                    let thinking = state.tag_buffer[..pos].to_string();
   539→                    emit_reasoning(thinking, &mut state.think_strip_leading_ws, events);
   540→                    state.in_think_block = false;
   541→                    state.visible_strip_leading_ws = true;
   542→                    let rest = state.tag_buffer[pos + "</think>".len()..].to_string();
   543→                    state.tag_buffer = rest;
   544→                    // Continue loop to process remaining content
   545→                },
   546→                None => {
   547→                    // Check if buffer ends with a prefix of "</think>"
   548→                    // to avoid emitting partial tag as reasoning text.
   549→                    let suffix_match = longest_tag_suffix(&state.tag_buffer, "</think>");
   550→                    if suffix_match > 0 {
   551→                        let safe = state.tag_buffer.len() - suffix_match;
   552→                        let emit = state.tag_buffer[..safe].to_string();
   553→                        emit_reasoning(emit, &mut state.think_strip_leading_ws, events);
   554→                        let kept = state.tag_buffer[safe..].to_string();
   555→                        state.tag_buffer = kept;
   556→                    } else {
   557→                        // No partial tag — emit everything as reasoning
   558→                        let buf = std::mem::take(&mut state.tag_buffer);
   559→                        emit_reasoning(buf, &mut state.think_strip_leading_ws, events);
   560→                    }
   561→                    break;
   562→                },
   563→            }
   564→        } else {
   565→            // Look for <think> to enter think mode
   566→            match state.tag_buffer.find("<think>") {
   567→                Some(pos) => {
   568→                    let visible = state.tag_buffer[..pos].to_string();
   569→                    emit_visible(visible, &mut state.visible_strip_leading_ws, events);
   570→                    state.in_think_block = true;
   571→                    state.think_strip_leading_ws = true;
   572→                    let rest = state.tag_buffer[pos + "<think>".len()..].to_string();
   573→                    state.tag_buffer = rest;
   574→                    // Continue loop to process remaining content
   575→                },
   576→                None => {
   577→                    // Check if buffer ends with a prefix of "<think>"
   578→                    let suffix_match = longest_tag_suffix(&state.tag_buffer, "<think>");
   579→                    if suffix_match > 0 {
   580→                        let safe = state.tag_buffer.len() - suffix_match;
   581→                        let emit = state.tag_buffer[..safe].to_string();
   582→                        emit_visible(emit, &mut state.visible_strip_leading_ws, events);
   583→                        let kept = state.tag_buffer[safe..].to_string();
   584→                        state.tag_buffer = kept;
   585→                    } else {
   586→                        // No partial tag — emit everything as visible
   587→                        let buf = std::mem::take(&mut state.tag_buffer);
   588→                        emit_visible(buf, &mut state.visible_strip_leading_ws, events);
   589→                    }
   590→                    break;
   591→                },
   592→            }
   593→        }
   594→    }
   595→}
   596→
   597→/// Return the length of the longest suffix of `text` that is a prefix of `tag`.
   598→///
   599→/// For example, `longest_tag_suffix("abc<th", "<think>")` returns 3 because
   600→/// `"<th"` is a 3-character prefix of `"<think>"`.
   601→fn longest_tag_suffix(text: &str, tag: &str) -> usize {
   602→    let text_bytes = text.as_bytes();
   603→    let tag_bytes = tag.as_bytes();
   604→    let max_check = text_bytes.len().min(tag_bytes.len());
   605→    for len in (1..=max_check).rev() {
   606→        if text_bytes[text_bytes.len() - len..] == tag_bytes[..len] {
   607→            return len;
   608→        }
   609→    }
   610→    0
   611→}
   612→
   613→/// Process a single SSE data line and return any events to yield.
   614→///
   615→/// This handles the common OpenAI streaming format used by:
   616→/// - OpenAI API
   617→/// - GitHub Copilot API
   618→/// - Kimi Code API
   619→/// - Any other OpenAI-compatible API
   620→///
   621→/// Content inside `<think>...</think>` tags is emitted as `ReasoningDelta`
   622→/// events rather than `Delta`, allowing the UI to show reasoning text
   623→/// separately. This handles models (DeepSeek R1, QwQ, MiniMax) that embed
   624→/// chain-of-thought in `content` rather than using `reasoning_content`.
   625→pub fn process_openai_sse_line(data: &str, state: &mut StreamingToolState) -> SseLineResult {
   626→    if data == "[DONE]" {
   627→        return SseLineResult::Done;
   628→    }
   629→
   630→    let Ok(evt) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(data) else {
   631→        return SseLineResult::Skip;
   632→    };
   633→
   634→    let mut events = vec![StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone())];
   635→
   636→    if let Some(usage) = parse_openai_compat_usage_from_payload(&evt) {
   637→        state.input_tokens = usage.input_tokens;
   638→        state.output_tokens = usage.output_tokens;
   639→        state.cache_read_tokens = usage.cache_read_tokens;
   640→        state.cache_write_tokens = usage.cache_write_tokens;
   641→    }
   642→
   643→    let delta = &evt["choices"][0]["delta"];
   644→
   645→    // Handle user-visible text content, stripping <think> tags.
   646→    if let Some(content) = delta["content"].as_str()
   647→        && !content.is_empty()
   648→    {
   649→        process_content_think_tags(content, state, &mut events);
   650→    }
   651→
   652→    // Some OpenAI-compatible backends stream planning text in
   653→    // `reasoning_content` or `reasoning`. Surface it separately so UI can
   654→    //  show it in the thinking area without polluting final assistant text.
   655→    let reasoning_text = delta["reasoning_content"]
   656→        .as_str()
   657→        .or_else(|| delta["reasoning"].as_str());
   658→    if let Some(reasoning_content) = reasoning_text
   659→        && !reasoning_content.is_empty()
   660→    {
   661→        events.push(StreamEvent::ReasoningDelta(reasoning_content.to_string()));
   662→    }
   663→
   664→    // Handle tool calls
   665→    if let Some(tcs) = delta["tool_calls"].as_array() {
   666→        for tc in tcs {
   667→            let index = tc["index"].as_u64().unwrap_or(0) as usize;
   668→
   669→            // Check if this is a new tool call (has id and function.name)
   670→            if let (Some(id), Some(name)) = (tc["id"].as_str(), tc["function"]["name"].as_str()) {
   671→                state
   672→                    .tool_calls
   673→                    .insert(index, (id.to_string(), name.to_string(), String::new()));
   674→                events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallStart {
   675→                    id: id.to_string(),
   676→                    name: name.to_string(),
   677→                    index,
   678→                });
   679→            }
   680→
   681→            // Handle arguments delta
   682→            if let Some(args_delta) = tc["function"]["arguments"].as_str()
   683→                && !args_delta.is_empty()
   684→            {
   685→                if let Some((_, _, args_buf)) = state.tool_calls.get_mut(&index) {
   686→                    args_buf.push_str(args_delta);
   687→                }
   688→                events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallArgumentsDelta {
   689→                    index,
   690→                    delta: args_delta.to_string(),
   691→                });
   692→            }
   693→        }
   694→    }
   695→
   696→    // Detect error finish reasons (e.g. "network_error", "content_filter").
   697→    // Normal reasons (null, "stop", "tool_calls", "length") are not errors.
   698→    if let Some(reason) = evt["choices"][0]["finish_reason"].as_str() {
   699→        match reason {
   700→            "stop" | "tool_calls" | "length" | "function_call" => {},
   701→            error_reason => {
   702→                events.push(StreamEvent::Error(format!(
   703→                    "Provider stream ended with finish_reason: {error_reason}"
   704→                )));
   705→            },
   706→        }
   707→    }
   708→
   709→    SseLineResult::Events(events)
   710→}
   711→
   712→/// Generate the final events when stream ends (tool call completions + done).
   713→///
   714→/// Any residual content in the think-tag buffer is flushed as the appropriate
   715→/// event type (reasoning if we were inside a think block, visible otherwise).
   716→pub fn finalize_stream(state: &mut StreamingToolState) -> Vec<StreamEvent> {
   717→    let mut events = Vec::new();
   718→
   719→    // Flush any remaining think-tag buffer content
   720→    if !state.tag_buffer.is_empty() {
   721→        let remaining = std::mem::take(&mut state.tag_buffer);
   722→        if state.in_think_block {
   723→            events.push(StreamEvent::ReasoningDelta(remaining));
   724→        } else {
   725→            events.push(StreamEvent::Delta(remaining));
   726→        }
   727→    }
   728→
   729→    // Emit completion for any pending tool calls
   730→    for index in state.tool_calls.keys() {
   731→        events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index: *index });
   732→    }
   733→
   734→    events.push(StreamEvent::Done(Usage {
   735→        input_tokens: state.input_tokens,
   736→        output_tokens: state.output_tokens,
   737→        cache_read_tokens: state.cache_read_tokens,
   738→        cache_write_tokens: state.cache_write_tokens,
   739→    }));
   740→
   741→    events
   742→}

   744→// ============================================================================
   745→// Responses API helpers (shared by openai.rs and github_copilot.rs)
   746→// ============================================================================
   747→
   748→/// Split system messages into `instructions` and convert the rest to Responses
   749→/// API `input` items.
   750→///
   751→/// The Responses API uses a top-level `instructions` field instead of a system
   752→/// message role.  This function extracts all system messages, joins them with
   753→/// `\n\n`, and converts the remaining messages via [`to_responses_input`].
   754→#[must_use]
   755→pub fn split_responses_instructions_and_input(
   756→    messages: Vec<ChatMessage>,
   757→) -> (Option<String>, Vec<serde_json::Value>) {
   758→    let mut instruction_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
   759→    let mut non_system: Vec<ChatMessage> = Vec::new();
   760→
   761→    for message in messages {
   762→        match message {
   763→            ChatMessage::System { content } => {
   764→                if !content.trim().is_empty() {
   765→                    instruction_parts.push(content);
   766→                }
   767→            },
   768→            other => non_system.push(other),
   769→        }
   770→    }
   771→
   772→    let instructions = if instruction_parts.is_empty() {
   773→        None
   774→    } else {
   775→        Some(instruction_parts.join("\n\n"))
   776→    };
   777→
   778→    (instructions, to_responses_input(&non_system))
   779→}
   780→
   781→/// Resolve the output index from a Responses API event.
   782→///
   783→/// The Responses API uses `output_index` for items and `index` for
   784→/// sub-item fields.  WebSocket events may also use `item_index`.
   785→/// Falls back to `fallback` if none of these keys are present.
   786→pub fn responses_output_index(event: &serde_json::Value, fallback: usize) -> usize {
   787→    event
   788→        .get("output_index")
   789→        .or_else(|| event.get("item_index"))
   790→        .or_else(|| event.get("index"))
   791→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
   792→        .map(|i| i as usize)
   793→        .unwrap_or(fallback)
   794→}
   795→
   796→/// State for tracking Responses API SSE streaming.
   797→#[derive(Default)]
   798→pub struct ResponsesStreamState {
   799→    /// Map from index -> (call_id, name)
   800→    pub tool_calls: HashMap<usize, (String, String)>,
   801→    /// Set of tool call indices that have already emitted `ToolCallComplete`.
   802→    pub completed_tool_calls: HashSet<usize>,
   803→    /// The next tool call index to assign.
   804→    pub current_tool_index: usize,
   805→    pub input_tokens: u32,
   806→    pub output_tokens: u32,
   807→    pub cache_read_tokens: u32,
   808→    pub cache_write_tokens: u32,
   809→}
   810→
   811→/// Process a single SSE data line from a Responses API stream.
   812→///
   813→/// Returns [`SseLineResult`] indicating whether to skip, yield events, or stop.
   814→///
   815→/// Handles the event types emitted by the Responses API:
   816→/// - `response.output_text.delta` → text delta + `ProviderRaw`
   817→/// - `response.output_item.added` (type=function_call) → tool call start + `ProviderRaw`
   818→/// - `response.function_call_arguments.delta` → tool call arguments delta + `ProviderRaw`
   819→/// - `response.function_call_arguments.done` → tool call complete + `ProviderRaw`
   820→/// - `response.completed` → parse usage, done
   821→/// - `error` / `response.failed` → error + `ProviderRaw`
   822→pub fn process_responses_sse_line(data: &str, state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> SseLineResult {
   823→    if data == "[DONE]" {
   824→        return SseLineResult::Done;
   825→    }
   826→
   827→    let Ok(evt) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(data) else {
   828→        return SseLineResult::Skip;
   829→    };
   830→
   831→    // Emit ProviderRaw for every parsed event, mirroring the Chat Completions path.
   832→    let raw = StreamEvent::ProviderRaw(evt.clone());
   833→
   834→    match evt["type"].as_str().unwrap_or("") {
   835→        "response.output_text.delta" => {
   836→            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
   837→                && !delta.is_empty()
   838→            {
   839→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::Delta(delta.to_string())])
   840→            } else {
   841→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
   842→            }
   843→        },
   844→        "response.output_item.added" => {
   845→            if evt["item"]["type"].as_str() == Some("function_call") {
   846→                let id = evt["item"]["call_id"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
   847→                let name = evt["item"]["name"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
   848→                let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index);
   849→                state.current_tool_index = state.current_tool_index.max(index + 1);
   850→                state.tool_calls.insert(index, (id.clone(), name.clone()));
   851→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::ToolCallStart { id, name, index }])
   852→            } else {
   853→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
   854→            }
   855→        },
   856→        "response.function_call_arguments.delta" => {
   857→            if let Some(delta) = evt["delta"].as_str()
   858→                && !delta.is_empty()
   859→            {
   860→                let index =
   861→                    responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
   862→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::ToolCallArgumentsDelta {
   863→                    index,
   864→                    delta: delta.to_string(),
   865→                }])
   866→            } else {
   867→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
   868→            }
   869→        },
   870→        "response.function_call_arguments.done" => {
   871→            let index = responses_output_index(&evt, state.current_tool_index.saturating_sub(1));
   872→            if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
   873→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index }])
   874→            } else {
   875→                SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw])
   876→            }
   877→        },
   878→        "response.completed" => {
   879→            if let Some(usage) = evt
   880→                .get("response")
   881→                .and_then(|response| response.get("usage"))
   882→            {
   883→                let parsed = parse_openai_compat_usage(usage);
   884→                state.input_tokens = parsed.input_tokens;
   885→                state.output_tokens = parsed.output_tokens;
   886→                state.cache_read_tokens = parsed.cache_read_tokens;
   887→                state.cache_write_tokens = parsed.cache_write_tokens;
   888→            }
   889→            SseLineResult::Done
   890→        },
   891→        "error" | "response.failed" => {
   892→            let msg = evt["error"]["message"]
   893→                .as_str()
   894→                .or_else(|| evt["response"]["error"]["message"].as_str())
   895→                .or_else(|| evt["message"].as_str())
   896→                .unwrap_or("unknown error");
   897→            SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw, StreamEvent::Error(msg.to_string())])
   898→        },
   899→        _ => SseLineResult::Events(vec![raw]),
   900→    }
   901→}
   902→
   903→/// Generate the final events when a Responses API stream ends.
   904→///
   905→/// Emits `ToolCallComplete` for any pending tool calls and a final `Done` with
   906→/// accumulated usage.
   907→pub fn finalize_responses_stream(state: &mut ResponsesStreamState) -> Vec<StreamEvent> {
   908→    let mut events = Vec::new();
   909→
   910→    let mut pending: Vec<usize> = state.tool_calls.keys().copied().collect();
   911→    pending.sort_unstable();
   912→    for index in pending {
   913→        if state.completed_tool_calls.insert(index) {
   914→            events.push(StreamEvent::ToolCallComplete { index });
   915→        }
   916→    }
   917→
   918→    events.push(StreamEvent::Done(Usage {
   919→        input_tokens: state.input_tokens,
   920→        output_tokens: state.output_tokens,
   921→        cache_read_tokens: state.cache_read_tokens,
   922→        cache_write_tokens: state.cache_write_tokens,

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the schema normalization module. Let me create a detailed summary document.

## Complete Schema Normalization Module Exploration

I've thoroughly explored the schema normalization module in the Rust codebase. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. **Files Related to Schema Normalization**

All files are in `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/tough-net/crates/providers/src/openai_compat/`:

- **`schema_normalization.rs` (329 lines)** - Core normalization logic
- **`provider.rs` (976 lines)** - Tool conversion functions that call the normalization
- **`strict_mode.rs` (186 lines)** - Strict mode patching for OpenAI compliance
- **`tests.rs` (953 lines)** - Comprehensive test suite
- **`mod.rs`** - Module exports

### 2. **The Warning Log Location**

The WARN log **"openai tool schema failed Draft 2020-12 preflight; using raw schema for best-effort normalization"** is emitted at:

- **File:** `crates/providers/src/openai_compat/schema_normalization.rs`
- **Line:** 260
- **Function:** `canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat()` (lines 242-286)

The warning is one of THREE fallback warnings in that function (lines 258-260, 267-270, 278-281).

### 3. **The Preflight Check** 

The preflight validation happens at lines 255-264:

```rust
let document = match SchemaDocument::from_json(&input) {
    Ok(document) => document,
    Err(error) => {
        warn!(
            error = %error,
            "openai tool schema failed Draft 2020-12 preflight; using raw schema for best-effort normalization"
        );
        return input;  // Fallback to raw schema
    },
};
```

**Key details:**
- The `$schema` URI is stripped BEFORE the preflight check (lines 251-253) so non-2020-12 drafts (e.g., draft-07) can be processed
- `SchemaDocument::from_json()` is the preflight validation from the `json_schema_ast` crate
- This validates the schema conforms to Draft 2020-12 JSON Schema structure
- If it fails, the function returns the raw (unsanitized) input as a fallback

### 4. **Best-Effort Normalization Fallback Path**

The "best-effort normalization" fallback occurs when canonicalization fails. There are actually THREE fallback conditions:

1. **Line 255-264:** `SchemaDocument::from_json()` fails (main preflight check)
2. **Line 266-272:** `document.root()` fails (canonical AST resolution)
3. **Line 274-285:** `document.canonical_schema_json()` fails (canonicalization generation)

All three return the raw `input` (the schema without `$schema` URI):

```rust
document
    .canonical_schema_json()
    .map_or_else(
        |error| {
            warn!(
                error = %error,
                "openai tool schema canonicalization was unavailable; using raw schema for best-effort normalization"
            );
            input  // Fallback
        },
        serde_json::Value::clone,
    )
```

The "best-effort" then continues with the raw schema being transformed by the `schemars` transforms (lines 294-328):
- `ReplaceConstValue`
- `ReplaceUnevaluatedProperties`
- `ReplacePrefixItems`
- `RemoveRefSiblings`
- `OpenAiSchemaSubsetTransform`
- `SimplifyCompositeTransform`
- `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform`
- `RestoreEnumTypeTransform`

### 5. **Main Normalization Function**

**`sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(schema: &mut serde_json::Value)` (lines 291-329)**

This is the public entry point that:
1. Calls `canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat()` to canonicalize with fallback
2. Converts result to `schemars::Schema`
3. Applies 8 recursive transforms
4. Converts back to `serde_json::Value`

The transforms handle:
- Converting `const` values to `enum`
- Removing unsupported keywords (if/then/else, dependentSchemas, patternProperties, etc.)
- Collapsing single-variant composites
- Stripping empty `{}` schemas
- **Pruning orphaned `required` entries** (issue #747) - crucial for Gemini compatibility
- **Restoring `type` annotations on enums** (issue #740) - required by Fireworks AI

### 6. **Call Chain: Who Calls the Normalization**

The normalization is called once per tool per request in these public functions:

**In `provider.rs`:**

1. **`to_openai_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value], strict: bool) -> Vec<serde_json::Value>` (lines 86-119)**
   - Iterates each tool
   - Clones `parameters` (line 90)
   - Calls `sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params)` (line 91)
   - Optionally calls `patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params)` (line 93)
   - Returns wrapped in Chat Completions format

2. **`to_responses_api_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value]) -> Vec<serde_json::Value>` (lines 135-168)**
   - Iterates each tool
   - Clones `parameters` (line 140)
   - Calls `sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params)` (line 141)
   - Always calls `patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params)` (line 142)
   - Returns wrapped in Responses API format

**Usage frequency:**
- **Called once per tool per LLM completion request** (not per session)
- The providers that use it:
  - `openai/provider/completion.rs` - OpenAI Chat Completions
  - `openai/provider/websocket.rs` - OpenAI WebSocket (Responses API)
  - `openai/provider/streaming.rs` - OpenAI streaming
  - `github_copilot/provider.rs` - GitHub Copilot (multiple endpoints)
  - `kimi_code.rs` - Kimi Code
  - `openai_codex.rs` - OpenAI Codex

### 7. **Comprehensive Test Coverage**

**`tests.rs` (953 lines)** contains 28 test functions covering:

**Core sanitization tests (lines 144-265):**
- `sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat_strips_recursive_unsupported_keywords` - validates keyword removal
- `sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat_recurses_into_array_form_items` - tuple/prefixItems handling

**Strict mode tests (lines 268-451):**
- `to_openai_tools_strict_mode_applied_by_default` - strict=true behavior
- `to_openai_tools_non_strict_skips_patching` - strict=false avoids array-form types
- `to_openai_tools_non_strict_complex_cron_like_schema` - nested object handling
- Enum-related tests (lines 407-515)

**Type restoration tests (lines 517-645):**
- `sanitize_restores_type_on_string_enum` - Fireworks AI regression fix
- `sanitize_restores_type_on_boolean_enum`
- `sanitize_restores_type_on_integer_enum`
- `sanitize_infers_number_for_mixed_int_float_enum`
- `sanitize_does_not_infer_type_for_mixed_enums`

**Orphaned required pruning tests (lines 670-810):**
- `sanitize_prunes_orphaned_required_entries` - issue #747
- `sanitize_prunes_orphaned_required_in_nested_objects`
- `sanitize_prunes_required_from_stripped_dependent_schemas`

**Draft-07 handling tests (lines 812-924):**
- `sanitize_draft07_schema_strips_unsupported_keywords` - issue #743
- `responses_tools_draft07_attio_schema_normalized` - end-to-end test

### 8. **Key Design Patterns**

**Immutable-style parameter handling:**
- Schemas are cloned in `to_openai_tools()` / `to_responses_api_tools()` before mutation
- Normalization occurs in-place on the clone
- Original tool data is never modified

**Fallback-first approach:**
- Canonicalization failures don't crash - they fall back to best-effort
- Three levels of fallback in `canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat()`
- Warnings are logged at each fallback for observability

**Transform composition:**
- Uses `schemars::transform` trait for composable schema transformations
- Applies transforms recursively to entire schema tree
- Custom transforms: `PruneOrphanedRequiredTransform`, `RestoreEnumTypeTransform`, `SimplifyCompositeTransform`
- Built-in transforms: `ReplaceConstValue`, `ReplaceUnevaluatedProperties`, etc.

**Provider-specific behavior:**
- OpenAI Chat Completions: conditional strict mode (line 229)
- Responses API: always strict mode (line 327)
- Non-strict providers (e.g., Google via OpenRouter) avoid array-form types to prevent rejection

### 9. **Issue Tracking**

The module fixes three major issues:
- **Issue #740:** Type annotations stripped by canonicalization (Fireworks AI regression)
- **Issue #743:** Draft-07 schemas with `not` keyword producing empty `{}` (Attio MCP tools)
- **Issue #747:** Orphaned `required` entries causing Gemini rejection (Home Assistant MCP tools)

1→//! Shared helpers for OpenAI-compatible streaming with tools.
     2→//!
     3→//! This module provides reusable functions for parsing OpenAI-style SSE streams
     4→//! that include tool calls. Used by openai.rs, github_copilot.rs, and kimi_code.rs.
     5→
     6→use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
     7→
     8→use {
     9→    moltis_agents::model::{
    10→        ChatMessage, CompletionResponse, StreamEvent, ToolCall, Usage, UserContent,
    11→        decode_tool_call_arguments,
    12→    },
    13→    serde::Serialize,
    14→    tracing::trace,
    15→};
    16→
    17→use super::{
    18→    schema_normalization::sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat,
    19→    strict_mode::patch_schema_for_strict_mode,
    20→};
    21→
    22→// ============================================================================
    23→// OpenAI Tool Schema Types
    24→// ============================================================================
    25→// These types enforce the correct structure for OpenAI-compatible APIs.
    26→// Using typed structs instead of manual JSON prevents missing fields at compile time.
    27→//
    28→// References:
    29→// - Chat Completions: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
    30→// - Responses API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/openai/how-to/responses
    31→// ============================================================================
    32→
    33→/// Chat Completions API tool format (nested under "function").
    34→///
    35→/// ```json
    36→/// { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "...", ... } }
    37→/// ```
    38→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    39→pub struct ChatCompletionsTool {
    40→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    41→    pub tool_type: &'static str,
    42→    pub function: ChatCompletionsFunction,
    43→}
    44→
    45→/// The function definition nested inside ChatCompletionsTool.
    46→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    47→pub struct ChatCompletionsFunction {
    48→    pub name: String,
    49→    pub description: String,
    50→    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    51→    pub strict: bool,
    52→}
    53→
    54→/// Responses API tool format (flat, name at top level).
    55→///
    56→/// ```json
    57→/// { "type": "function", "name": "...", "parameters": {...}, "strict": true }
    58→/// ```
    59→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    60→pub struct ResponsesApiTool {
    61→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    62→    pub tool_type: &'static str,
    63→    pub name: String,
    64→    pub description: String,
    65→    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    66→    pub strict: bool,
    67→}
    68→
    69→/// Convert tool schemas to OpenAI Chat Completions function-calling format.
    70→///
    71→/// Uses the nested `function` object format required by Chat Completions API:
    72→/// ```json
    73→/// { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "...", ... } }
    74→/// ```
    75→///
    76→/// When `strict` is `true`, patches schemas for strict mode compliance:
    77→/// - `additionalProperties: false` on all object schemas
    78→/// - All properties included in `required` array
    79→/// - Originally-optional properties made nullable via array-form types
    80→///
    81→/// When `strict` is `false`, schemas are sanitized but not patched for strict
    82→/// mode. This avoids array-form types like `["boolean", "null"]` that
    83→/// non-OpenAI backends (e.g. Google via OpenRouter) reject.
    84→///
    85→/// See: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling>
    86→pub fn to_openai_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value], strict: bool) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
    87→    let result: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tools
    88→        .iter()
    89→        .filter_map(|t| {
    90→            let mut params = t["parameters"].clone();
    91→            sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params);
    92→            if strict {
    93→                patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params);
    94→            }
    95→
    96→            let name = t["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
    97→            let description = t["description"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
    98→
    99→            // Use typed struct to ensure all required fields are present
   100→            let tool = ChatCompletionsTool {
   101→                tool_type: "function",
   102→                function: ChatCompletionsFunction {
   103→                    name: name.clone(),
   104→                    description,
   105→                    parameters: params,
   106→                    strict,
   107→                },
   108→            };
   109→
   110→            trace!(tool_name = %name, strict, "converted tool to Chat Completions format");
   111→
   112→            // Serialize to Value for compatibility with existing API
   113→            serde_json::to_value(tool).ok()
   114→        })
   115→        .collect();
   116→
   117→    trace!(tools_count = result.len(), "to_openai_tools complete");
   118→    result
   119→}

content.text:
Now I have a clear understanding. The issue has two parts:

1. **Root cause**: `canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat` only strips `$schema` from the top level. If a nested schema (e.g., inside `definitions`) also has `$schema`, `json_schema_ast` rejects it. More broadly, even with root stripping, the `warn!` fires for every schema on every call.

2. **Log level**: The `warn!` level is wrong for an expected fallback that works correctly. CLAUDE.md says `debug!` for expected-but-not-configured states.

Let me write a failing test first, then fix.

Now I'll add the test and fix. The key insight: `canonicalize_schema_for_openai_compat` only strips `$schema` from the root, but `json_schema_ast`'s `validate_schema_dialects` recursively validates `$schema` at all levels. I also need to verify that draft-07 schemas actually get canonicalized (not just fallback-processed).

fix(providers): strip $schema recursively and downgrade fallback log level (#760)

The schema normalization module logged a WARN on every tool schema that
used a non-Draft-2020-12 `$schema` URI (e.g. draft-07 from Attio MCP
tools). Two problems:

   schemas with `$schema` inside nested `definitions`/`$defs` would
   still fail canonicalization and fall through to the WARN path.

2. The fallback log level was `warn!`, but this is an expected state
   for non-standard schemas where the fallback works correctly. Per
   the project's logging conventions, `debug!` is appropriate for
   "detailed diagnostics" rather than "unexpected but recoverable."

Fix: replace root-only stripping with `strip_schema_keyword_recursive`
that removes `$schema` from all levels, and downgrade the three fallback
log statements from `warn!` to `debug!`.

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of the changes:

**Issue**: `schema_normalization` logged `WARN` 1000+ times per session for every tool schema with a non-Draft-2020-12 `$schema` URI (like draft-07 from Attio MCP tools).

**Root causes**:
1. `$schema` was only stripped from the root of the parameters object. `json_schema_ast` validates `$schema` recursively at every nesting level, so schemas with `$schema` inside nested `definitions`/`$defs` still failed canonicalization.
2. The fallback was logged at `warn!` level, but it's an expected state (not an error) since the fallback works correctly.

**Fix** (`schema_normalization.rs`):
- Added `strip_schema_keyword_recursive()` that removes `$schema` from all levels of the JSON tree, not just the root
- Downgraded all three fallback log statements from `warn!` to `debug!`

**Tests** (`tests.rs`):
- `sanitize_draft07_nested_definitions_schema_canonicalized` — verifies schemas with `$schema` inside nested `definitions` go through full canonicalization (not fallback), by checking for the boolean→enum lowering side-effect
- `sanitize_draft07_schema_uses_canonicalization_not_fallback` — verifies root-level draft-07 `$schema` also triggers canonicalization, not fallback
